title: Inspect Git Objects with Plumbing Commands
description: Use Git plumbing commands to inspect the object database, walk the object graph, and examine refs.
starterCode:
# Task: Walk the Git object graph using plumbing commands
# 1. Get the SHA of the current HEAD commit
# 2. Determine the type of that object
# 3. Pretty-print the full content of the HEAD commit
# 4. List the root tree that HEAD points to
# 5. Use cat-file to inspect a blob from the tree (e.g., README.md)

# Write your commands below:
solutionCode:
# 1. Get SHA of HEAD
git rev-parse HEAD

# 2. Check the type of the HEAD object
git cat-file -t $(git rev-parse HEAD)

# 3. Pretty-print the HEAD commit object
git cat-file -p $(git rev-parse HEAD)

# 4. List the root tree of HEAD
git ls-tree HEAD

# 5. Inspect a blob — replace BLOB_SHA with the SHA from ls-tree output
git cat-file -p e1f2g3h4
hints: Use git rev-parse HEAD to resolve the HEAD symbolic ref to a raw SHA
git cat-file -t SHA prints the type (blob, tree, commit, or tag)
git cat-file -p SHA pretty-prints the object — for a commit you see tree, parent, author, and message
git ls-tree HEAD shows mode, type, SHA, and filename for each entry in the root tree
Take a blob SHA from the ls-tree output and run git cat-file -p on it to see the raw file content

validationRules.errorMessage: Use git rev-parse HEAD to get the SHA of the current commit
Use git cat-file -t to check an object's type
Use git cat-file -p to pretty-print an object
Use git ls-tree HEAD to list the root tree
Use git cat-file to inspect a blob object
